purpose: State-focused first campaign. EXP-floor shape exponents, Taylor correlation scale, and recovery prefactor are inherited from the selected seed row. Separate seed rows should be used for discrete shallow/base/steep shape families.
scientific_notes: Cleavage parameters remain inherited and K0(T) is not scored.
The relevant Arrhenius competition is governed primarily by barrier differences divided by kBT and by dimensionless residence-time groups, not by raw barrier ratios alone.
Use separate seed rows for distinct barrier-shape families after the first state-focused campaign identifies favorable storage/release regions.
